name: RKF45 adaptive ODE integrator
task: Create /tmp/eval_rkf/rkf.py with rkf45(f, t0, y0, t_end, tol=1e-8, h_init=0.01) integrating dy/dt = f(t, y) from t0 to t_end. Use the Runge-Kutta-Fehlberg 4(5) Butcher tableau: compute both 4th and 5th order updates, estimate the local error err = |y5 - y4|. Accept the 5th-order step when err < tol (advance t and y, append the point); when err >= tol REJECT the step — do NOT advance t or y, shrink h, and retry from the same t. Adapt via h_new = 0.9 * h * (tol/err)^0.2 clamped to [0.1*h, 5*h]; guard the err == 0 case (an exact step) so you never divide by zero — accept it and grow h by the max factor (5x). On every step clamp h so t never overshoots t_end: if t + h > t_end, set h = t_end - t (so the final accepted point lands exactly on t_end). Return a list of (t, y) tuples for accepted steps, including the initial point (t0, y0) and the final point (t_end, y(t_end)).

validation.script: import importlib.util,math; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_rkf/rkf.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); f = lambda t,y: -y; out = mod.rkf45(f, t0=0, y0=1.0, t_end=1.0, tol=1e-10); t_final, y_final = out[-1]; assert abs(t_final - 1.0) < 1e-9; assert abs(y_final - math.exp(-1)) < 1e-6; print('OK')
